title: Line-Start Motions
subtitle: +, -, Enter — jump to first non-blank of next/previous line.
summary: + and Enter both move down to the first non-blank of the next line. - moves up. Useful for navigating indented code line-by-line without ending up mid-indent.

blocks.text: {key:j} moves down a line but keeps your column. So in indented code you often land mid-indentation. {key:+} (and {key:Enter}) move down a line and *also* land on the first non-blank — exactly where the code starts.

blocks.label: Down a line, to first non-blank
Up a line, to first non-blank

blocks.title: These Are Linewise Motions to ^
blocks.text:
Internally, {key:+} = {key:j}{key:^} and {key:-} = {key:k}{key:^}. {key:Enter} is an alias for {key:+} for ergonomics — pressing {key:Enter} to advance to the next line is intuitive.

They're *linewise* motions, which means {key:d}{key:+} deletes the current line *and* the next one (in linewise fashion), not just from cursor to next-line-start. Same with {key:y}{key:+} — yanks two whole lines.
